behaviors.description: Keyword-frequency analyzer assigns correct sentiment for reviews containing only configured positive/negative words.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the local scoring logic (token counting + thresholds). No real I/O or model behavior is required, and mocks are unlikely to hide core arithmetic/decision bugs here.
behaviors.test_description: Call the scoring function directly with injected positive/negative dictionaries and thresholds. Use table-driven cases (all-positive, all-negative, neutral-by-threshold, punctuation/case variants) and assert exact sentiment output.

behaviors.description: Explanation analyzer references specific phrases from the review rather than generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real language generation quality. A mocked explanation service could always return phrase-containing text and miss real model drift/boilerplate behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer on reviews with distinctive phrases, then assert the explanation includes or clearly paraphrases review-specific evidence (not generic template text). Repeat across multiple prompts/reviews to detect stochastic regressions.

behaviors.description: For mixed positive/negative keyword reviews, sentiment follows configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is deterministic rule implementation (equal counts / threshold boundary). This is local decision logic; mocks do not materially improve failure detection.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke scoring logic with crafted mixed-signal inputs that land exactly on tie conditions and near-boundaries. Inject each tie-break config mode and assert the returned sentiment matches policy.

behaviors.description: Explanation for sarcastic text identifies negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: This is semantic intent detection quality, which depends on real model behavior and can vary by run/model version. Mocked analyzers would likely hide true sarcasm failures.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real explanation analyzer on sarcastic samples (e.g., 'Great, another broken feature') and assert output attributes negative intent with evidence from context, not literal-positive interpretation. Run multiple times/variants to catch non-deterministic misses.

behaviors.description: Single response returns both score and explanation, and explanation sentiment direction matches score.
behaviors.justification: Response assembly is integration-level, but semantic alignment check depends on real explanation content. Mocking explanation can mask real mismatch between deterministic score and generated rationale.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real end-to-end API with representative reviews. Assert response includes both fields in one payload, then verify explanation sentiment direction (positive/negative/neutral) is consistent with returned score for each case.
